name: Ted Curson
abstract: Theodore Curson (June 3, 1935 – November 4, 2012) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: WLIB Festival of Music and Drama
Newport Jazz Festival: George Coleman Octet/ Ted Curson Sextet/ Anthony Braxton Quintet
Tribute to Charles Mingus: Mingus Music
Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: My first gig in New York was, I think, a Monday night at Birdland with my brother and Ted Curson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, it was Ted Curson. I don’t mind saying who it is, the trumpet player, Ted Curson. I’m sure it was an event that he wouldn’t remember. To him it was probably just a silly off comment, like teasing a young woman.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: Eric Dolphy and a guy named Ted Curson, trumpet player, both respectively were going to quit Mingus and get their own groups.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Ted Curson was running a jam session in the eighties. I mean, he didn’t go to an academy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
